steps.title: Use the two zeros
steps.content:
The graph passes through $(10,0)$ and $(-6,0)$, so the zeros are $10$ and $-6$. Therefore
$$f(x)=a(x-10)(x+6)$$

steps.title: Find b in terms of a
steps.content:
Expand the factors:
$$f(x)=a(x^2-4x-60)=ax^2-4ax-60a$$
Comparing this with $ax^2+bx+c$ gives
$$b=-4a$$

steps.title: Test the possible value
steps.content: Then $$a+b=a-4a=-3a$$ The value $-6$ is possible because $$-3a=-6$$ $$a=2$$ and $2$ is an integer greater than $1$. The answer is D.
